VSCode 1.69 Broke `! + Tab`? Here's the Fix
After VSCode updated to 1.69, the classic
HTML expansion stopped working. Here's the fix.
Since VSCode 1.69, the frontend dev's favourite
shortcut no longer brings up any suggestion popup.
html emmet suggestion not automatically display
This is a real annoyance for dev work, so here's a quick fix.
That's a lot to type, so here's another trick. Open Settings.
This setting enables "press Tab to expand Emmet abbreviations", which means
The interesting catch is that the dropdown suggestion popup still doesn't show up — that'll probably get fixed in a future VSCode release.
Side note: you can also add this to your
language-json shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
Now the default scaffolded HTML will use your specified lang and UTF-8, like this:
language-html shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Document</title>
</head>
<body>
  
</body>
</html>
